description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:19.03.2 image with a docker:19.03.2-dind service and sets global variables DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2 and DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR=/certs. Define three stages: test, prepare, and build. In the prepare stage add a prepare_2019_1_3f1 job that runs the shell command ci/prepare.sh, sets variables BUILD=f1, DOWNLOAD_URL=https://beta.unity3d.com/download/dc414eb9ed43/UnitySetup-2019.1.3f1, SHA1=ea97ef4c42180bd833e5b31b56f083b727a99cc8, TAG=2019.1.3f1, VERSION=2019.1.3, retries twice, and uploads licence_request.alf as an artifact. In the test stage add a test-ci-generator job that uses the python:3.7-alpine image, runs a before_script sequence (cd ci-generator, python -V, pip install virtualenv, virtualenv venv, source venv/bin/activate, pip install -r requirements.txt), caches .cache/pip and ci-generator/venv/, then executes coverage run -m unittest tests/test*.py, coverage report, and coverage html, stores the ci-generator/htmlcov directory as an artifact, and extracts coverage with the regex /^TOTAL.*\s+(\d+\%)$/. In the build stage add six unity_2019_1_3f1-* jobs (android, ios, linux-il2cpp, mac, webgl, windows) that inherit the .build template (retry 2, script ci/build.sh), each with its own COMPONENTS, TAG, and other Unity‑specific variables, and all run after a global before_script that logs into $CI_REGISTRY using $CI_REGISTRY_USER and $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD. No explicit rules or deployment targets are defined, so jobs run on all branches.
